score.description: All existing high-priority agent-accessible capabilities remain intact with no new gaps. The change is a strictly additive internal fallback that preserves the existing function contract.

summary: The diff adds a Vault fallback to getDecryptedApiKeyForProvider() in src/lib/aiConfiguration.ts. The function's public contract (signature, return type, async behavior) is unchanged. The Vault fallback only activates when local decryption would have previously returned null — it is a recovery path, not a replacement. The readCredential dependency uses non-interactive Supabase Edge Function calls that degrade gracefully when unauthenticated. No new auth requirements, no browser-only APIs, no changes to agent tool implementations.

observations.title: Vault fallback may prolong resolution time in edge cases
observations.details: When local decryption fails, the Vault fallback adds one Supabase Edge Function round trip (~50-200ms) before returning null. This only affects the null-returning code path and is a strict improvement over the previous behavior of returning null immediately. The latency is negligible relative to the LLM API call that follows.

function_contract.return_semantics: unchanged — returns decrypted key string or null
function_contract.async_behavior: unchanged — already async; Vault fallback adds one more await in the null-returning branch only

auth_requirements.degradation: When no Supabase user is present, readCredential returns null immediately — the fallback is a no-op and the function returns null (same as before)

interactive_behavior.readCredential: non-interactive — calls supabase.auth.getUser() (in-memory JWT check) + supabase.functions.invoke() (HTTP fetch). No DOM prompts, no window.confirm, no user interaction required.

caller_compatibility.all_other_callers: all use await; no change needed

test_compatibility.providerKeyStorage.test.ts:195-209: test expects null on bad encrypted data — still passes because vaultCredentials readCredential returns null without an authenticated Supabase user

agent_integration.mcp_tools: none defined at app level — this codebase uses LLM features (noteQA, tutor, quiz gen) via Vercel AI SDK streaming, not MCP tool definitions
agent_integration.ai_features: all AI features go through factory.ts -> getDecryptedApiKeyForProvider(); the change is transparent to them
agent_integration.system_prompt: static system prompts in noteQA.ts:208-215 and other AI features; not affected by this change

what_works_well: The self-healing re-encrypt pattern (lines 535-536) improves future reads without changing the function contract
All error paths are logged with console.warn and degrade to null (non-throwing)
The Vault fallback is gated behind hasEncryptedData check, avoiding unnecessary network calls when no key was ever configured
readCredential is designed for non-interactive use — returns null on any failure, never throws
The change is fully backward compatible — existing tests should pass without modification
